name: Modify: change a field in an existing entry
description: Update a single JSON field in any existing src-data file (opens a PR for review)
title: [EMD] Modify: [do not edit]

field_guidance.folder:
**Data folder**

Pick the folder that contains the file you want to change.

If you're not sure which folder a given entry lives in, browse `src-data` on GitHub.
field_guidance.filename:
**Existing filename**

The `@id` of the file you want to change. With or without the trailing `.json` — the handler will add it if missing.

**Example:** for `model/canesm5-1.json` enter `canesm5-1`.

If the file does not exist the submission will fail.
field_guidance.key:
**Field name**

The JSON key to update. Nested keys are supported with dot notation, e.g. `metadata.version`.

**The key must already exist on the file** — if it does not, the submission will fail. Use the relevant stage form to add new fields.
field_guidance.value:
**New value**

Write the new value as you want it to appear in the JSON. Examples:

- A string: `My new description`
- A list: `["https://doi.org/...", "https://doi.org/..."]`
- A number: `2024`
- A boolean: `true`
- An object: `{"key": "value"}`

If the value parses as JSON it is stored as that type; otherwise it is kept as a string.
